title: paraleucothoe keiskei
intro: Paraleucothoe keiskei, also known as the Japanese keiskei fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, particularly in Korea and Japan, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Paraleucothoe keiskei include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and roots, which can be dried, powdered, or infused in hot water to create teas or decoctions for medicinal use.
